prompt: I'm a data engineer, never made a game before. I want to make a pixel art platformer but I keep thinking of features — wall jumping, double jump, enemies, bosses, power-ups, collectibles, a level editor, online leaderboards... Can you help me scope this down to something I can actually build in one session with Claude Code and Godot MCP?

assertions.text: Uses three-tier scope (MUST HAVE / SHOULD HAVE / OUT OF SCOPE)
Explains what a core loop is
Identifies core mechanic (movement/jump) as first thing to validate
Provides reasoning for each cut feature (not just 'cut')
Uses encouraging/supportive tone for a beginner
Suggests naming the game or asks for a name
Bridges game dev concepts to engineering terms (data engineer audience)
Mentions writing a GDD as a next step
